preamble: Shift of a sibilant affricate back to a palatal stop, often seen as a reversal or a specific type of palatalization in certain dialects.

examples.note: Variations where affricates shift back toward more palatalized articulations.
Certain dialects where the sibilant component is reduced in favor of a palatal stop.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
